repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md Section 3.1 has Manski bounded-outcome wrappers only, not CH/MSM/covariate-shift support penalties

published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020 + ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 + AdjahoChristensen2025 supply the component sensitivity languages; the displayed common support-function normalization is mostly convex duality

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: sharp joint propensity/outcome misspecification value interval and non-rectangularity witness, not a three-language scalar calibration frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Closest verified papers are KallusZhou2021 and JinXuZhengZhou2025 for MSM policy regret, CinelliHazlett2020 for partial-R2 sensitivity, AdjahoChristensen2025 for external-validity policy choice; none states the rank-one iff frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 has an open-set non-rectangularity witness for a different joint value-interval program

published_prior_art.cite: DornGuoKallus2025 concerns sharp ATE sensitivity bounds under MSM, not cross-language policy-score separation; the current witness is still elementary norm-geometry after Theorem 1

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses closer CH/MSM calibration-adjacent prior art: Soriano-Ben-Michael-Bickel-Feller-Pimentel 2023 on interpretable sensitivity for balancing weights and Huang-Pimentel 2023 on R2-parameterized variance-based sensitivity for weighting estimators.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship; after Theorem 1 the iff frontier is essentially equality of finite norm ratios, so flagship tier needs a sharper observable frontier between named published criteria, not just local support-function algebra.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The separation witness does not quote a specific published workflow that actually translates CH partial-R2 values into MSM or covariate-shift policy rankings; the current target is an informal shortcut.

soundness_flags.label: Assumption 5 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The ratio ||M_{pi1}||_2/||M_{pi2}||_2 can be undefined if the denominator is zero; state positive projection norms or compare cross-products instead.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Soriano et al. 2023 and Huang-Pimentel 2023 as explicit closest prior-art checks, then state exactly why their weighting-sensitivity calibrations do not imply the multi-policy regret frontier.

Replace the local rank-one slogan with a computable observable diagnostic: a finite matrix of policy scores/projections and an explicit all-pairs ratio test separating CH, MSM, and covariate-shift rankings.

To target flagship tier, tie the non-equivalence claim to at least two named published criteria, such as JinXuZhengZhou2025 MSM robust welfare and AdjahoChristensen2025 externally valid policy choice, and quote the exact workflow whose scalar calibration would fail.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison and upgrade the kernel from local norm-ratio algebra to a named, observable estimator-frontier result before resubmitting for flagship review.
